title: Smartsheet vs Things for Busy professionals
meta_description: For busy professionals needing less daily friction, pick Smartsheet when organizing work requires multi-level project hierarchies; Things fails first overall.
constraint_lens: You need a tool that lets you understand and manage complex work structures quickly without piecing things together manually.

one_second_verdict.summary: Best for busy professionals who need less daily friction.
one_second_verdict.reason: Things fails first when organizing work requires multi-level project hierarchies beyond simple task lists.

verdict.summary: Smartsheet lets you build multi-level hierarchies with parent rows, nested subtasks, and grouped phases, so you can structure complex projects in one place. Things is designed around flat lists with limited grouping, which works for simple task tracking but not layered project planning. For busy professionals managing complex work, this means more time trying to mentally map structure that is not represented in the tool. Smartsheet reduces that effort by making the hierarchy visible and organized.
verdict.decision_rule: If organizing work requires multi-level project hierarchies beyond simple task lists, Things fails first.

sections.heading: Why Smartsheet fits busy professionals
sections.content: You are organizing projects with multiple layers like phases, tasks, and subtasks, and need to see that structure clearly. Tools that flatten everything into simple lists force you to reconstruct the hierarchy in your head. Smartsheet fits this by letting you nest and group work visually, while Things keeps tasks in simpler structures that do not scale to complex project layouts.

bullets.point: Indented row hierarchy with parent and child relationships
bullets.why_it_matters: You can nest tasks under phases and break work into multiple levels, making complex structures visible at a glance.

bullets.point: Expandable and collapsible task groups within sheets
bullets.why_it_matters: You can hide or show levels of detail quickly, which helps you focus without losing the overall structure.

bullets.point: Grid-based layout that aligns tasks, subtasks, and phases in one view
bullets.why_it_matters: You can see all levels of a project in a single table, reducing the need to switch views or mentally track structure.

bullets.point: Simple project and list structure with minimal nesting
bullets.why_it_matters: You can manage tasks quickly without dealing with complex hierarchies, which keeps daily use fast for simpler work.

bullets.point: Quick task entry with minimal required fields
bullets.why_it_matters: You can add tasks instantly without structuring them into multiple levels, reducing setup time.

bullets.point: Clean interface focused on individual task completion
bullets.why_it_matters: There are fewer elements to manage on screen, which makes it easier to focus when your work does not require deep structure.

items.fails_when: You only need to track simple tasks and do not need multiple levels of hierarchy or structured planning.
items.what_to_do_instead: Switch to Things to manage tasks quickly without building or maintaining a layered structure.

items.fails_when: You need to organize work into phases, tasks, and subtasks but cannot represent that structure clearly in the tool.
items.what_to_do_instead: Use Smartsheet to create multi-level hierarchies and view the full structure in one place.

sections.heading: When this verdict might flip
sections.content: If your projects stay simple and do not require multiple levels of structure, Things becomes the better choice because it is faster to use without managing hierarchies.

sections.heading: Quick decision rules
rules: Use Smartsheet if your projects require multiple levels like phases and subtasks.
Use Things if your tasks can stay in simple lists without deep structure.
Avoid Things if you need to represent complex project hierarchies clearly.

faqs.q: Which tool better matches this priority?
faqs.a: Smartsheet fits this need better because Smartsheet indented row hierarchy with parent and child relationships. Things fails first when organizing work requires multi-level project hierarchies beyond simple task lists.

faqs.q: When should I choose Things instead?
faqs.a: Choose Things over Smartsheet when You only need to track simple tasks and do not need multiple levels of hierarchy or structured planning. Otherwise, Smartsheet remains the better fit for this comparison.

faqs.q: What makes Things fail first here?
faqs.a: Things fails first here when organizing work requires multi-level project hierarchies beyond simple task lists. That is the point where Smartsheet becomes the stronger pick.

faqs.q: Is this verdict only about one feature?
faqs.a: No. Smartsheet beats Things because Smartsheet indented row hierarchy with parent and child relationships, while Things loses once organizing work requires multi-level project hierarchies beyond simple task lists.
